pages.raw_text:
The 100000 clubs campaign to
raise $4,000 with which to pur-
chase a site and build new Home
for the Friendless will begin Thurs
day, March 26.


This date was set at today's
meeting of the club committee ap-
pointed to take charge of the pro.
ject.


On Wednesday, March %, the
club hopes to secure the attendance
of at least 500 interested citizens
at the noon luncheon. The final de-
tails will then be attended to and
committees named. 1t Is the inten-
ton to have enough of these COM
mittees to cover the entire city in
one days canvass.


The preparatory arrangements
'SAave been left in the hands of Sec.
Jones, of the club. and A. V.
Broderick.


The women's clubs of the city,
working in conjunction with
Mesdames Lewis. Clifford and
Smith. have agreed to provide the
furnishings for the new building.


The Home of the Friendless is
purely local institution. 1t was
organized in Spokane for the pur-
pose of caring for the homeless,
and is subject to no outside man.
agement.


At present it Is housing about so
children, AZ Of whom are absolute
ly dependent upon the home for
their daily bread and clothes. The
others are maintained at a low
price in the home by widowed
parents.


These children are fed. clothed
and sent to school. No child is re-
fused home whenever there is
room. But accommodations are
greatly overtaxed and the home is
a rickety old frame structure that
would probably be deathtrap in
case of fire. 1t cannot be aban-
doned because there is no other
place to house its charges.


That, and the immense pOssibili-
ties before the society for future
benevolent work in behalf of home
less children, is the reason the
10000 club has taken hold of the
project of procuring new and ade-
Quate building with its customary
vim and vigor.


There Is no more deserving
charitable project before the city
today than this. Its success means
safe and secure home, clothing
and care for boys and girls who
otherwise would be left to drift
along through poverty into crime.


The records of the home are not
public property, but an investiga-
ton of them will show an amount
of benefit conferred on the COM
munity in the years of its exist
ence, the magnitude Of which is not
even suspected by the people Of
this city.
